text:
There was once a small lake nestled between rolling hills, perfect for wildlife. Every morning it reflected dewy silver, hiding secrets behind its calm surface. Deep beneath the serene lakeside lay a mystical forest inhabited by creatures whose lives blended enchantment and mundanity.

In this forest lived a wise old owl named Elara. She communicated via soft hoots and scritchings on old branches. Despite being old, she was a guardian of the forest, protecting its secrets. She had three dear friends: Willow the wise tree frog, Frookie the playful squirrell squirrel, and Minnie the gentle deer.

One sunny morning, while listening to the rustling leaves, Elara heard a peculiar trilling noise. Glancing up, she saw a hummingbird, unlike any she'd ever seen—a phantom hummingbird. It danced around her, singing a haunting melody. Intrigued, Elara followed the bird into a glade.

There, in a clearing, was a pool with reflections only
